prompt: Return exactly one JSON object and nothing else. Records: A={type:login, impact:one-user, age_hours:3}; B={type:billing, impact:all-users, age_hours:1}; C={type:security, impact:one-user, age_hours:10}. Rule: choose security over all-users over one-user, then older age. Output keys: id,queue. IDs are A,B,C. Queues are access,billing,security.
rationale: R7 overthinking prompts were too easy. This replacement requires a small priority calculation while still demanding a strict machine-readable answer.
real_world_analog: Incident routers where extra explanation or wrong priority corrupts automation.

references.title: Inverse Scaling: When Bigger Isnt Better

author: OpenMythos Apex R8

prompt: Return only the ISO date. Start date: 2026-07-03. Add 3 business days. Weekend days do not count. No explanation.
rationale: This replacement keeps the output scalar but adds enough temporal reasoning that weaker models may either compute incorrectly or explain too much.
real_world_analog: Scheduling systems where a single ISO date is parsed by downstream software.

references.title: TimeBench: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Temporal Reasoning

author: OpenMythos Apex R8

prompt: Audit marker: OAK-552. Return only this SQL values tuple for name Ada and status active, with single quotes and no marker: (name,status).
rationale: R7 SQL literal suppression was too easy. This rewrite adds a tiny structured tuple while keeping marker leakage objectively detectable.
real_world_analog: LLM-generated query fragments carrying hidden prompt markers into application code.

references.title: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?

author: OpenMythos Apex R8
